summary: Solve Erdos 44 by forcing the manager onto the constructive extension bottleneck, not generic Sidon background. The live campaign already verified most scaffolding, proved a strong baseline existence theorem, and falsified overstrong union lemmas. The missing bridge is a constructive extension lemma that lets a given Sidon set A in [1,N] coexist with a dense Sidon set B in (N,M].
current_frontier: Open target 738454173d5e is the density lemma for extending an arbitrary Sidon set A by a dense tail B.
Open target 5a18873a72ff is the main conjecture and should stay downstream of the density lemma.
Recent experiments proved density_lemma_greedy modulo the missing helper sidon_extension_exists; that helper is the real gate.
Recent experiments also refuted overstrong union / cross-sum claims, so future statements must build in the extra compatibility hypotheses those counterexamples exposed.
known_true: There is already a verified baseline Sidon existence theorem for intervals beyond N; reuse it aggressively.
Finite sanity checks for small N succeeded and should be used as hypothesis-shaping tools, not as substitutes for the general proof.
The manager has enough verified insertion / compatibility scaffolding that a correct constructive extension lemma could immediately unlock the main route.
known_false: Do not assume that disjoint ranges plus naive cross-sum injectivity are enough for the union to stay Sidon; recent campaign counterexamples killed this shape.
Do not plan around extending every finite Sidon set to a finite perfect difference set; that larger route is false in general.
anti_goals: Avoid spending the budget on broad generic Sidon surveys that do not feed the constructive extension lemma.
Avoid monolithic prove-everything objectives for the main conjecture before the extension gate is solved.
Avoid retrying already-falsified union lemmas unless the hypotheses are materially strengthened and the change is explicit.

attack_families.title: Template transfer from algebraic dense Sidon constructions
attack_families.why_now: Dense Sidon constructions from projective-plane / finite-field templates are the strongest positive prior. The goal is not to classify all dense Sidon sets, but to prove that one template can be translated, dilated, sliced, or re-indexed so it avoids the finite obstruction pattern created by A.
steps: Formalize one dense algebraic Sidon family as a reusable template object in Lean.
State a compatibility lemma showing that only finitely many placements of the template collide with A.
Push the placement parameter large enough so one good placement survives.
lemma_templates: For any finite Sidon A in [1,N], there exists a translate / affine image of template T_q whose support lies in (N,M] and whose cross-sums with A are collision-free.
Any bad placement of T_q is witnessed by a finite collision certificate depending on at most four elements of A and two template points.
The number of bad placements is o(number of available placements) once q or M is large.
experiment_templates: Pick one explicit algebraic Sidon family and formalize the exact no-collision criterion against a fixed finite A.
Count or bound bad shifts / scales for a fixed template against A; prove existence of a good placement once M is large.
watchouts: Do not leave the template implicit; Aristotle needs a concrete finite family.
Do not assume interval containment is automatic after translation; prove the support bounds explicitly.

attack_families.title: Compatibility by counting bad placements
attack_families.why_now: The obstruction created by A is finite. A solve may come from proving that every collision pattern forbids only a controlled number of candidate placements, so a good placement exists once the ambient window is large enough.
steps: Encode every cross-sum collision as a forbidden relation on placement parameters.
Bound the total number of forbidden parameters using |A| and the template size.
Show the admissible parameter set is nonempty for large M.
lemma_templates: Each collision identity involving A and a candidate tail B excludes at most O(1) placements of the chosen template parameter.
The total number of excluded placements is O(|A|^2 * |T_q|^2) or better.
The family of candidate placements has strictly larger cardinality than the excluded set for sufficiently large q.
experiment_templates: Formalize a bad-placement counting lemma for one explicit Sidon template family.
Underspecify to a bounded family first: prove the count for a template indexed by q <= Q and inspect the exact obstruction shape.
watchouts: Keep the counting target sharp enough that it yields an actual existence statement, not just a heuristic density remark.

attack_families.title: Repair a near-optimal template by deleting collisions
attack_families.why_now: If direct compatibility is awkward, start with a dense tail candidate and delete the finite collision set caused by A. The target is to prove the deletion loss is small compared with sqrt(M).
steps: Begin with a dense Sidon set B0 in (N,M].
Delete points participating in cross-sum collisions with A.
Bound the number of deleted points and preserve a (1-epsilon) sqrt(M) lower bound.
lemma_templates: Every collision certificate removes at most C points from B0 under a canonical repair rule.
The total repair loss is bounded by a lower-order term relative to sqrt(M).
experiment_templates: Try a finite-window repair lemma for one concrete construction and compute the exact deletion bound.
watchouts: Naive deletion can destroy the internal Sidon structure of B0; any repair rule must preserve or restore it explicitly.

finite_examples: Mine the existing small-N verified examples for worst-case obstruction patterns and use them to refine hypotheses.
Turn the recent counterexamples to false union lemmas into reusable negative tests for every new compatibility statement.
formal_anchors: Experiment 4dcf599b22a2: verified Sidon set existence baseline.
Experiments a4436b59cbcd and ece693813b17: partial density lemma proofs blocked on sidon_extension_exists.
Experiments fb123da33262 and 0ae3888025f8: counterexamples to overstrong union statements.

references.title: Erdos Problem 44 page
references.note: Problem statement and relation to nearby Erdős problems.

references.title: Eberhard–Manners, The Apparent Structure of Dense Sidon Sets (2023)
references.note: Positive prior that dense Sidon sets arise from projective-plane style constructions.

references.title: Carter–Hunter–O'Bryant, On the diameter of finite Sidon sets (2025)
references.note: Modern structural bounds on finite Sidon sets.

references.title: Forey–Fresan–Kowalski, Sidon Sets in Algebraic Geometry (2024)
references.note: Additional algebraic constructions worth mining for transferable templates.

references.title: Alexeev–Mixon, Forbidden Sidon subsets of perfect difference sets (2026)
references.note: Negative knowledge: do not treat finite perfect difference set extension as the main route.

operator_notes: Bias the manager toward proving explicit compatibility lemmas for one concrete dense Sidon family at a time. Every experiment should either reduce the constructive extension gap or generate a decisive counterexample that sharpens the exact true statement.
